source_title: Loi visant à prévenir les effets nocifs de la boisson énergisante sur la santé des jeunes, 2026, chapitre 11
population: Prospective buyers of covered energy drinks
limitations: States the legal threshold, not compliance behaviour or health effect.
notes: Sections 1 and 2 prohibit sale to and purchase by persons under 16.

source_title: Loi visant à prévenir les effets nocifs de la boisson énergisante sur la santé des jeunes, section 3
limitations: Does not specify a universal challenge age or measured rejection rate.
notes: When asked, a buyer must use government or public-body photo ID showing name and date of birth; the seller must refuse if identity is not established.

source_title: Loi visant à prévenir les effets nocifs de la boisson énergisante sur la santé des jeunes, section 4
population: Internet, vending, and other non-co-present sales
limitations: Future regulations may create exceptions and alternative verification methods.
notes: Remote and automated sales provisions take effect with the first regulation under section 4.

source_title: Loi visant à prévenir les effets nocifs de la boisson énergisante sur la santé des jeunes, section 15
limitations: The remote-sales provisions have a different commencement rule.
notes: Most provisions enter into force on 2026-12-11.

source_title: L'encadrement des boissons énergisantes caféinées pour la santé et le mieux-vivre des adolescents
population: Youth in the cited 2022-2023 source data
limitations: Consumption prevalence is not the share of retail purchase attempts made by people under 16.
notes: INSPQ reports one youth in 16 consumed a sweetened caffeinated energy drink weekly.

source_title: L'encadrement des boissons énergisantes caféinées pour la santé et le mieux-vivre des adolescents
population: Youth in the cited 2022-2023 source data
limitations: Daily consumption prevalence is not a transaction frequency distribution.
notes: INSPQ reports one youth in 100 consumed a sweetened caffeinated energy drink daily.

source_title: L'encadrement des boissons énergisantes caféinées pour la santé et le mieux-vivre des adolescents
population: Youth affected by a sales ban
limitations: Identifies plausible substitution and informal-market effects but does not quantify them for Quebec.
notes: INSPQ recommends considering substitution to other harmful products and informal markets.

source_title: Comment se porte la santé de la population québécoise?
limitations: Age band includes lawful adults and does not measure purchase attempts or covered products exactly.
notes: ISQ reports 10 percent consumed energy drinks at least weekly.

unit: share consuming weekly or daily categories
source_title: Nouveaux indicateurs sur les jeunes de 15 à 29 ans
limitations: The source reports 10 percent at 1-6 times weekly and 2 percent at least daily; it does not identify under-16 transactions.
notes: Useful for bounding frequency, not directly for the modeled minor share.

population: Caffeinated energy drinks sold in Canada
limitations: A per-serving federal limit is not an estimate of Quebec purchase behaviour.
notes: Health Canada restricts caffeine from all sources to 180 mg per serving.

limitations: A mandatory caution statement is not a sales prohibition or outcome estimate.
notes: Labels must state that the products are not recommended for people under 14.

source_title: Face Analysis Technology Evaluation: Age Estimation and Verification
population: Subjects represented in NIST evaluation datasets
limitations: Performance varies by algorithm, dataset, image quality, age, sex, and region. Published thresholds do not directly estimate a Quebec retail threshold of 16.
notes: Use algorithm-specific, threshold-specific tables rather than a generic accuracy claim.

source_title: Assessing whether and how to use age assurance
population: Websites and online services using age assurance
limitations: Written for online services, not specifically Quebec energy-drink retail.
notes: Age assurance should be risk-based, proportionate, and no more information should be collected than necessary.

source_title: Assessing whether and how to use age assurance
population: Users subject to online age assurance
limitations: Does not quantify false rejection, retention, or disclosure rates.
notes: Guidance calls for purpose limitation, destruction as soon as possible, anti-linkability, appeals, user options, and infrequent authentication.

limitations: General digital identity guidance, not a Quebec age-token implementation or empirical cost estimate.
notes: Useful for threat modeling, assurance levels, federation, and wallet architecture.

source_title: WCAG 2.2 Understanding Success Criterion 3.3.8: Accessible Authentication (Minimum)
population: People using authentication systems, including people with cognitive disabilities
limitations: Applies to authentication design and does not estimate age-assurance abandonment rates.
notes: Authentication must not require a cognitive function test without an alternative or assistance mechanism.

source_title: Le taux général du salaire minimum passera à 16,60 $ l'heure le 1er mai 2026
population: Workers paid the general minimum wage
limitations: Minimum wage is not total loaded labour cost and does not measure time per verification.
notes: Useful as a transparent lower-bound context for staff-time costing.

source_title: Evaluation of the Guaranteed Income Supplement, Phase 2
population: Sample of non-tax-filing seniors who were GIS eligible or receiving GIS
limitations: Highly selected older population, not representative of Quebec adult energy-drink buyers.
notes: Twenty-six percent reported not having picture ID. Included only to demonstrate that ID access barriers can be substantial in some groups.

unit: share of assessed age controls circumvented
source_title: Commission d’accès à l’information participation in GPEN youth privacy sweep
jurisdiction: International sweep reported by Quebec regulator
limitations: Website and app controls are not retail energy-drink transactions; the unit is systems assessed, not user attempts.
notes: Useful only as context for weak self-declaration and circumvention, not as a direct model estimate.

limitations: Not an empirical estimate. Included to exercise the model and expose evidence priorities.
notes: Replace or bound with direct evidence before policy use.
